item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, OP, does Iris have the abilities described in both the SCP-105 article and this one? It'd make that very interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted for correct use of "quantum" in its correct sense of "god only knows what the fuck".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1465: It's quantum-encrypted lumelinguistics, doc. That shit don't make sense.

aaaaaaand upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Daughter of Thaumas" ("The Miraculous One"; a minor Olympian sea god) -one of the goddess Iris' epitaths.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What does 'thaumantos' mean though?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this. It is much better than the original Iris.

On a side note, I wonder whether this Iris would be able to glean any information from photographs of or taken by light-based SCPs. For instance SCP-2108. It would be very interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As someone going through the mainlist in order, it took me reading the comments to catch the connection to 105, but that added such a new level to both of them.

Also, while checking 105, I noticed that if I don't upvote 105, these have the same rating, and I find that it suits perfectly, but it deserves the upvote >.>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think that if she didn't have her ability she could be a valuable asset to the Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hate to make this discussion sound like a broken record, but I would've liked this more as a 105 rewrite. An ARC'd 105 would be acceptable, but the name drop at the end just makes this meh with 105 still standing. Hell, if 105 were ARC'd and replaced with something other than this, and this stayed where it's at, it'd be great. Sorry to see that this project didn't turn out as planned, Djoric. Upvoted, but not headcanon'd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: upvote after reading the comments

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nice. I think the best compliment I can pay your scientific neologisms is that they made so much sense, I didn't realize how many of them there had been until other people commented!
Obviously, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why this wasn't used as the rewrite for 105 is something I have trouble understanding. This is a superior article in every way. Well at least the numbers don't lie. This sits at over 50 points higher than 105. Excellent skip.

item.title: Re: This made my brain hurt...
item.content:encoded: Yes it is.

item.title: This made my brain hurt...
item.content:encoded:
…but in a good way. Nice job with the technosciencebabble, and I loved this line:

It’s all very quantum.

and

It's quantum-encrypted lumelinguistics, doc. That shit don't make sense.

Also - "lumelinguistics" - is that a Latin-esqe portmanteau meaning "language of light"?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is one of those articles that I'd like to see developed into a book.

Other than that I have no complaints. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just think, would I be the kind of person to add L-space to the Foundation universe?

Of course I am.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Of course the Foundation Archives are trans-dimensional.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmmm…
If the girls are quantum twins, then either one of them is in the wrong continuum or the Foundation archives are trans-dimensional. The 2nd requires the least suspension of disbelief gymnastics, at least I find it easier to accept information leakage than physical transfer without the "slider" Iris noticing. But it raises interesting complications for the usability of the database. How can I, standing outside a cell door reading a file, be sure that it isn't really the write-up from universe Gamma-C and wholly unsuited for our version of the skip?

I think the tidier explanation is that the Phoenix patterns were a time-reversed indication of the life-changing events 1465 would have in Phoenix. Namely, being contained by the Foundation. Her relationship to 105 could be as simple as sisters separated at birth. Or more metaphysical, they are the human equivalent of entangled quantum particles; many properties related or identical but "spinning" in opposite directions.

Thanks for the nifty new brain-toy! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I do tend to agree that this would have worked best as a meta-rewrite-replacement for 105 with the original ARC'ed, but at the same time I understand why people wanted to keep classic!Iris around on the mainlist. I have upvoted; right now it feels like it stands alone, but possibly that's just because I know the thought processes behind it and how it was originally going to be used on the site. I think there's a danger that over time it's going to seem less comprehensible to new readers, but possibly I'm overthinking things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is amazing. I think having it as a counterpart to 105 is what really makes this work.

Here's a thought: If her eyes are already pretty damaged from staring at bright lights, it's probably only a matter of time before she can't get her vision back, right? Maybe she's a bit more self-controlled with her abilities now that she knows her days are counted?

PS:If other people share the same headcanon as Lumancer and I, then that means I won't be the first to write a Marianas-themed SCP. :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dammit, I keep mixing Iris up with the reality bender. I'm surprised a rewrite was deemed necessary though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It just dawned on me that Phoenix, Arizona is important. What is a Phoenix? A bird made of fire. Of light. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When I read it, I interpreted it as future 1465 trying to warn her past self about the Foundation and now she's caught in a time loop where she's responsible for her own capture.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You definitely nailed the "I'm obsessed and drank too much coffee" aspect, like a paranoid conspiracy theorist without the conspiracy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, but as a semi-standalone or counterpart, not a rewrite.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You'll be at it a while, I made up the jargon. And the quantum stuff…it's quantum. Best to leave it at that.

Not seeing the valley girl here. Workaholic savant with a motor mouth, yes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll have to re-read this with a Google tab open to get any of the science whatsoever. The only thing I'd suggest is not writing Iris as a Mean Girls character, she's a bit too Valley Girl.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As I said on chat, as a rewrite of 105 this doesn't work, but as a standalone I like it just fine. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it! It's been a long time since I looked at 105, it's held up surprisingly well, although with what I imagine to be a lot of rewrites. I like this better, but part of me thinks they really work as a duo.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was supposed to be a rewrite of SCP-105 with references to otherside Iris being references to the original 105. It was deemed too different and put into its own slot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I made it by myself, mainly to illustrate that she had been making up the terms herself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reading the comments, I feel like I'm missing something. Overall, I didn't think it was all that interesting, up until the very end, which, while intriguing in its own right, was also a tad dense to figure out. Just enough for a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is fantastic. Headcanon: 105 is pre-Marianas-event Iris, this is post-Marianas-event Iris.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this one can coexist with 105. Do you realize the possibilities? Well, on the bad side, I had to read really slowly to learn and understand the lumino jargon you were using (you made it by yourself or there's actually any kind of scientific facts behind it?). Anyways, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this as a standalone. I am sorry that you were psyched up to put it into the 105 slot, but honestly, I like it even more this way. Think of the story possibilities — whether or not it's even actually alternate universe. Both options are quite promising. (I guess your pain is my glee? :( Sorry dude)

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: GAZE INTO THE LIGHT

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I still say this would've worked as a rewrite to 105. Sorry it had to turn out like this, man.

Still, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Liked it in chat, loved helping out with the development (what little help I gave) and approve of it being posted, though I disagree that it goes /too/ far from the original. I think this might be the first "official" alternate-universe version of a skip that we've had.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Apparently this was too far from the original concept for it to be a rewrite. Whatevs.
